architecture_fit.note: Single attention head, MLP dropped — a minimal delta from base_model.py that exactly expresses mirror-position comparison; AUC 1.0 at every slice.

baseline_comparison.note: Histogram/ridge baseline measured under identical conditions sits at ~0.52 everywhere; charted side-by-side against the mirror head.

faithfulness.note: Two routing ablations (identity, off-by-one shift) collapse the same circuit to chance, showing the mirror routing is the load-bearing component.

operating_range.note: Holds at AUC 1.0 across the full k=1..8 difficulty sweep, but only one seq_len/vocab is tested — no probe of larger scales where it might strain.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Every weight set by hand with zero training; the circuit is written out explicitly and reproduces perfect separation.

visual_judgement.note: Attention heatmap shows the anti-diagonal routing directly, AUC-by-k bar chart contrasts head vs baseline vs ablation, plus a worked example — claim is legible at a glance.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties each panel to the goal: rank-AUC y-axis (exact metric), hardest k=1 on x-axis, and the mechanism shown not just described.

notes: An exemplary hand-built attempt: a single position-only attention head wired i -> L-1-i checks mirror-token equality, achieving perfect AUC at every difficulty including the diagnostic k=1, while the histogram baseline sits at chance. Faithfulness is strong via routing ablations that kill the mechanism, and the visualisation cleanly shows both the routing and the separation. Only modest gap is operating range, which is confined to the fixed seq_len/vocab task with no larger-scale stress test.
